description:
Goal: make the Compiler2 backend interpreter handle the runtime intrinsics needed for spawned/message-passing/resource programs instead of erroring at the extern seam.

Signal: Compiler2 interpreter runs can execute programs that reach `fz_spawn`, `fz_spawn_opt`, `fz_send`, `fz_self`, and `fz_make_resource` through `BackendProgram` without falling back to old planner state or returning "not supported yet" errors.

Change:
- extend `call_lowered_extern` for the Compiler2 backend path to reuse the existing interpreter runtime hooks for scheduler/resource intrinsics
- add Compiler2 backend-interpreter tests that cover spawn/send/self and resource creation
- keep the artifact contract unchanged: the interpreter still consumes only `BackendProgram` + runtime state

Why:
- `fz-rh2.8.2.2` proves the backend artifact path for quicksort, Enum.reduce, and variadic externs, but the general entry-point switch remains blocked until the backend interpreter can run the runtime-graph programs that depend on these intrinsics

Checks:
- Compiler2 backend interpreter tests cover spawn/send/self/resource behavior
- no legacy planner/type-resolution fallback is reintroduced
title: Compiler2 backends: support scheduler and resource runtime intrinsics in backend interp
